# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.
# Category: container_security

# pulls node from dockerhub (the default version) when the container runs
FROM node:latest   # Node v14 as of now, check with latest LTS versions if you want a specific one 

WORKDIR /app       # sets working directory for our app's code and dependencies. Default is current path where docker command was issued in context by default .i e it would be ./ on your machine   (e g., cd into project folder before running the Dockerfile) when we define workdir, node commands that follow will run inside /app so there’s no need to specify absolute paths.
    # Copies package dependencies for both development and production environment from a requirements file  which is in .dockerignore (ignores these files with exclusion of __pycache__)   - not using env/ but required by default when creating container or building image if you use it there should be nothing to exclude, the app does have its own.
    COPY package*.json ./         # Copies all *package* related JSON (including dev dependencies) files from "current" directory into workdir's "/app".  - not using env/ but required by default when creating container or building image if you use it there should be nothing to exclude.
    EXPOSE 3000                  # This is a declaration that tells the Docker server on which port(ports) we want our app and database services accessible at from outside world (default).   - not using env/ but required by default when creating container or building image if you use it there should be nothing to exclude.
    
    # Installing dependencies  in production environment only, here installs npm packages that are present into /app directory while starting up the docker service with CMD command for running app on a defined port (3000). No exclusion of package-lock and then any node related env variables/settings should be used to run your application
    RUN npm install  # Installing dependencies. - not using env/. Use `npm ci` if there are .env files in the project for environment variable overriding or installation specifics, use NODE_ENV=production react-scripts start otherwise default node mode will serve our app but with --port specified to avoid conflict on port number
    
    # Copies over any build artifact such as index.html  This is where we are pointing npm "start" script which should be started from Dockerfile (in docker run command). It's not an env variable or setting like the other ones, but rather points to starting point of app in development environment
    COPY --from=0 /app/build /usr/share/nginx/html      # Copies our production build artifact from working directory onto nginx server. - Not using .env as not specified here whether you have a specific Dockerfile, CI setup etc to set it up for your use-case
    
    CMD ["node", "server.js"]                  # When docker is started in the running container this command will be executed and tells nginx server on which port we should serve our app from its public folder (usually `/usr/share/nginx/html`). Defaults to being 3028 if not defined when Docker start.
